notes: Trade-risk screening preserves jurisdiction separation and does not borrow MRLs or reference values across authorities.

A pass status only means all applicable governed limits that were actually found were met for that jurisdiction; inspect MRL and reference-value coverage statuses before treating the lane as complete.
Inconclusive jurisdictions remain active review signals, not implicit passes: CODEX_GLOBAL, US.

reviewPrompts.prompt: Treat the US lane as review-required until the missing or partial MRL coverage is explicitly accepted.
Use `mrlCoverageStatus` for US to distinguish partial curated scope, anchor-only posture, and explicit gaps instead of flattening them into a generic missing-limit state.
Use `referenceValueJurisdictionStatus` for US to confirm whether the reference-value side is exact, anchor-only, family-curated-without-value, or an explicit gap.
Treat the CODEX_GLOBAL lane as review-required until the missing or partial MRL coverage is explicitly accepted.
Use `mrlCoverageStatus` for CODEX_GLOBAL to distinguish partial curated scope, anchor-only posture, and explicit gaps instead of flattening them into a generic missing-limit state.

limitations.message: Trade-risk review bundle supports governed screening review and audit handoff only; it is not a final market-clearance or regulatory acceptance decision.

notes: Bundle packages a governed trade-risk screening result with explicit coverage semantics, review prompts, and pinned review resources.
No-borrowing semantics are preserved: missing jurisdiction coverage remains explicit and is not silently substituted from another authority.
Use this bundle for internal trade-screening review and audit handoff, not as a market-clearance or regulator-acceptance packet.
Trade-risk screening preserves jurisdiction separation and does not borrow MRLs or reference values across authorities.

A pass status only means all applicable governed limits that were actually found were met for that jurisdiction; inspect MRL and reference-value coverage statuses before treating the lane as complete.
Inconclusive jurisdictions remain active review signals, not implicit passes: CODEX_GLOBAL, US.
Review-required status means at least one jurisdiction remains failed, invalid, inconclusive, or coverage-limited under the shipped MRL/reference-value scope.
Illustrative review note for trade-risk bundle testing.
